concerns.text: Section 5.3 (Short-Context Performance Preservation): The decision to adopt 0% short data is based on a single run per ratio. No standard deviations, confidence intervals, or statistical tests (e.g., paired t-test) are reported to confirm that the 0.99 point drop in short-context AVG (66.47 vs 65.48) is not within the noise margin of the evaluation protocol. Add error bars or a significance statement.

Section 5.2 (Data Mixture): The 8:2 ratio is selected as 'best' based on a single point estimate (57.70). The difference between 8:2 (57.70) and 6:4 (57.27) is 0.43 points. Without reporting variance across seeds or runs, this selection is statistically indistinguishable from random fluctuation. Report variance or justify the selection with a significance test.

Section 6.2 (Generalization): The claim of 'strong performance' at 256K/512K relies on a single evaluation run. The paper does not report confidence intervals for the extrapolated scores (55.09, 52.52). Given the high variance typical in long-context inference, a single point estimate is insufficient to support the 'generalization' claim. Add error bars or multiple-seed averages.

Appendix A.3 (mRoPE Base Frequency): The ablation compares three base frequencies. The text claims 'comparable performance' between 2e6 and 4e6 but does not provide statistical evidence (e.g., overlapping confidence intervals) to support this. The differences in specific tasks (e.g., 56.86 vs 56.02) are small; without variance, the conclusion is weak.
